Where It Begins

Every addition starts as a drawing.

Before a single shovel hits the ground, we sit down with you and design the space. We work with local architects and engineers who know Lorain County zoning, setbacks, and soil conditions. What you see on paper is what gets built.

No surprises. No scope creep. Just a clear plan you approve before we move.

Your Home, As It Stands

We start by understanding what you already have.

Every addition project begins with a thorough walkthrough of your existing structure. We evaluate the foundation, the framing, the mechanicals. We're building on your home, not around it.

22+
Years in NE Ohio
340+
Additions completed
97%
On-time delivery
Site Preparation

The disruption is temporary. The result is permanent.

We know excavation looks alarming. Your yard gets torn up, equipment shows up at dawn, and for a stretch it looks like nothing is going right. We've done this enough times to promise: it's supposed to look like this.

We protect your existing landscaping, maintain safe access to your home throughout, and clean up every day before we leave.

Foundation

Built to last, tied to what's already there.

The foundation of your addition is engineered to integrate with your existing structure. We don't cut corners on concrete depth, anchor bolt placement, or drainage. Lorain County winters demand a foundation done right.

"I've had two contractors tell me the foundation work on my addition was the best they'd seen. These guys are serious."
— Avon Lake homeowner, 2023
Framing

The skeleton rises. You start to see it.

Framing is the moment a homeowner first believes it. The walls go up, the roof takes shape, and suddenly the blueprints are standing in your backyard. Our crews are fast, precise, and coordinated with your mechanical subs from day one.

We frame tight because every gap in framing is a future problem in drywall, trim, or energy efficiency.

Rough-In

The work you'll never see. The work that matters most.

Electrical, plumbing, HVAC. The rough-in phase is invisible once drywall goes up, but it's where the quality of your addition lives. We use licensed subs we've worked with for years. Code inspections pass. Every time.

  • 01Electrical panel review and service upgrade if needed
  • 02Plumbing tied into existing supply and drain lines
  • 03HVAC zoned and balanced with your existing system
  • 04Insulation to current Ohio energy code
Dried In

Weathertight. The turning point of every project.

When the house wrap goes on and the windows are set, something changes on the job site. The addition starts to look like a room instead of a construction zone. The pace picks up. Everyone knows the finish line is in sight.

We stage our window orders early so there's no gap between framing and dried-in. Time inside a schedule is money out of your pocket, and we treat it that way.

Exterior Complete

Siding matched. Roofline tied in. Almost there.

The siding, soffit, fascia, and roofing on your addition are matched to your existing home. We don't settle for close enough. If we have to special-order a discontinued profile, we do it. The addition should look like it's always been there.

"Our neighbors thought we moved. They didn't recognize the house."
— North Ridgeville homeowner, 2022
The Finish Line

Looks like it was always there.

From the first conversation to the final landscaping, we manage every phase of your addition. Lorain County families have trusted us with their homes for over two decades.
